result:
All five functions are implemented in `problems.py`:

- **`parse_duration`** — regex-based parse enforcing h→m→s order, requiring at least one component, rejecting bad types/whitespace/duplicates/trailing garbage.
- **`luhn_valid`** — strips spaces/hyphens, validates all-digit, applies the standard Luhn doubling algorithm.
- **`flatten`** — recursive flatten that skips empty nested dicts, leaves non-dict values (including lists) untouched, doesn't mutate input.
- **`top_k_frequent`** — `Counter` + sort by `(-count, word)`, sliced to `k` (empty list for `k <= 0`).
- **`rolling_max`** — monotonic deque of indices, O(n), verified on 2M elements in ~0.7s.

Verified against all docstring examples plus edge cases (empty/invalid input, ties, mutation checks, large-input performance) with throwaway scripts — no test files left behind.
